channel.title: Posts on The Codex Arunel
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on The Codex Arunel

item.title: A Deep Dive Into Python - zip()
item.description: Table Of Contents Introduction Declaration Basics Details Conclusion Resources Introduction The zip() function in Python takes an input of two or more of the basic collections in Python; the list, set, tuple and dict. It returns an iterator (sequence) of tuples, where the first tuple contains the first elements of all the inputs, the second contains the second elements and so on. We’ll see how to use the Python zip() function in this article.
